message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_linalg_inv autograd via apply_function with -Y^T grad Y^T backward [bd-frq7]

First child of frankentorch-tw0y (linalg severed-autograd umbrella).
tensor_linalg_inv used to extract values, run the kernel, and
rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with the standard matrix-calculus
backward
    grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T   where Y = A^{-1}
derived from dY = -A^{-1} dA A^{-1}. Y is saved for backward so we
don't recompute the inverse. The matmul chain is implemented
inline in the backward closure (n×n is small enough; no need to
wire through tensor_matmul which would require a fresh session
context).

Adds linalg_inv_propagates_gradient_via_inv_transpose_outer
regression test: A = diag(2, 4) so Y = diag(1/2, 1/4); under sum
loss, grad simplifies to -y_i * y_j outer product, giving
    grad_A = -[[1/4, 1/8], [1/8, 1/16]]
exactly. Asserts to within 1e-12.

Closes frankentorch-frq7. ft-api lib tests: 1140 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -85,6 +85,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-f6nn","title":"ft-nn PReLU and shrink activations sever autograd","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs after the recent activation-gradient repair wave. Several activation modules still read tensor values, compute in plain f64, and return session.tensor_variable(..., false), so a requires_grad=true input produces a fresh untracked output and tensor_backward cannot propagate gradients. Concrete sites: PReLU::forward lines 3576-3642 reads both input and its learnable weight via tensor_values and returns an untracked leaf, so both input gradients and the PReLU weight gradient are dropped; Softshrink lines 3918-3938, Hardshrink lines 3966-3978, Tanhshrink lines 3991-4000, Softsign lines 4014-4023, Softmin lines 4046-4057, and RReLU lines 4154-4168 have the same severed-input pattern. Existing tests for these modules are forward-only (for example prelu_default_forward/prelu_has_parameters/prelu_multichannel, softshrink_shrinks, tanhshrink_values, softsign_bounded, softmin_is_softmax_of_negated, rrelu_module_*), and the full ft-nn test suite still passes: rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn reported 508 passed on 2026-04-29. Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, run PReLU::forward (or any listed activation), reduce with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for input is absent because the forward output is a requires_grad=false leaf. For PReLU, tensor_gradient for self.weight is also absent even though parameters() exposes that learnable slope. Fix by composing each activation through existing autograd-aware primitives (e.g. PReLU via tensor_where/tensor_mul with broadcasted weight; shrink ops via tensor_where; Softmin via tensor_neg or 0-input subtraction then tensor_softmax; RReLU via leaky_relu-style composition) and add regression tests asserting input gradients, plus weight gradients for PReLU.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:09:37.892015821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.745564147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.743783997Z","close_reason":"Composed all 7 listed activation modules through autograd-aware primitives: PReLU via tensor_reshape+tensor_expand+tensor_mul+tensor_where (now propagates to both input and learnable weight); Softshrink/Hardshrink via nested tensor_where + tensor_abs/tensor_sub/tensor_add + tensor_gt/tensor_lt; Tanhshrink via tensor_sub(input, tanh(input)); Softsign via tensor_div(input, 1+abs); Softmin via tensor_neg + tensor_softmax; RReLU via where(x>=0, x, slope*x). Added 8 gradient regression tests asserting analytical d/dx for each module (plus PReLU weight grads, both single-slope and multichannel). All 11 existing forward tests continue to pass — forward parity preserved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-fntr","title":"softmax / log_softmax lack subprocess conformance against torch/numpy","description":"tensor_softmax and tensor_log_softmax are the canonical activation/output functions for classification heads, attention layers, and most loss formulations. They have unit tests in ft-api but no subprocess conformance against an authoritative reference (torch.nn.functional.softmax / scipy.special.softmax / numpy). Bit-exact agreement matters because:

- Numerical stability: log_softmax(x) = log(softmax(x)) is computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid exp overflow; the equivalence with the naive form must hold within libm precision across all inputs.
- Gradient stability: softmax/log_softmax forward/backward are coupled (dL/dx = softmax(x) - target for cross-entropy). Drift between FrankenTorch and PyTorch here corrupts every classification training run.
- Edge cases: large positive inputs (exp overflow risk), large negative (underflow), uniform input (all equal probabilities), single-element softmax (degenerate).

Add torch_softmax_torch_subprocess_conformance covering interior, large-magnitude, near-uniform, and edge-case inputs along multiple dims; assert ULP-tolerant equality against scipy.special.softmax (or numpy formulation if scipy unavailable). Same for log_softmax.

Bias toward filing-from-/reality-check: this is a pre-existing instance test gap, not a new bug.","notes":"Closed by adding torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering interior, uniform, scalar, large-positive / large-negative (with max-subtract numerical stability), mixed-sign, and 1-D / 2-D / 3-D shapes across multiple dims. 8-ULP tolerance with 1e-15 absolute floor. Also surfaced the log_softmax precision bug tracked under frankentorch-ebrb (now fixed in the same commit).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:22:44.964172677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.625884901Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.622244776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-fq9","title":"Add trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner to ft-api","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:31:01.289432681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.881175699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.880929192Z","close_reason":"Implemented trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner with 9 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-frq7","title":"Implement tensor_linalg_inv autograd: grad_A = -A_inv^T grad_out A_inv^T [child of tw0y]","description":"Wrap tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the standard matrix-calculus backward:
    grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T   where Y = A^{-1}
Save Y (the forward output) for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse. Output shape is [n, n] for n×n input. F64-only autograd path; F32 input with requires_grad continues to fail-loud (consistent with the affine_grid pattern).

Add tensor_linalg_inv_propagates_gradient regression test in ft-api: for diagonal A, the inverse is reciprocal-diag and the gradient under sum loss simplifies to a clean closed-form for verification.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the analytical backward grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T where Y = A^{-1}. Y is saved for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse; the matmul chain is implemented inline in the closure (n×n is small enough). Added linalg_inv_propagates_gradient_via_inv_transpose_outer regression test (diagonal A=[[2,0],[0,4]] under sum loss → grad = [-1/4,-1/8,-1/8,-1/16]). ft-api lib tests: 1140.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:04:39.351961113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:06:46.241906978Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:06:46.239539378Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-tf4","title":"Add complex number ops: real, imag, conj, complex, view_as_real, view_as_complex","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T04:21:59.828290129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:28:01.820179670Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T18:28:01.819933384Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-tk5","title":"Enrich Module register_parameter/register_buffer errors with module type context","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found that Module::register_parameter() and Module::register_buffer() default implementations (ft-nn/src/lib.rs) return `Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported { operation: "register_parameter" })`.

The error tells you WHAT operation failed but not WHICH module rejected it. When debugging a deep Sequential model with 50+ layers, "register_parameter unsupported" gives no clue about which layer is the problem.

## Current error type
```rust
pub enum ModuleRegistrationError {
    InvalidName { kind: &'static str },
    NameConflict { name: String },
    Unsupported { operation: &'static str },
}
```

## Design decision: How to get the module type name in a trait default

The trait default method signature is `fn register_parameter(&mut self, ...)`. We CANNOT call `std::any::type_name::<Self>()` because the default method has `Self: ?Sized` (it's on `dyn Module`).

**Solution**: Add a new required method to the Module trait:
```rust
fn module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str;
```
This is cheap (one &'static str per impl), gives correct type info even through dyn dispatch, and follows the same pattern PyTorch uses with `_get_name()`.

Each module implementation returns its name: "Linear", "Conv2d", "Sequential", etc.

The default for register_parameter/register_buffer then becomes:
```rust
fn register_parameter(...) -> Result<(), ModuleRegistrationError> {
    Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported {
        operation: "register_parameter",
        module_type: self.module_type_name(),
    })
}
```

## Plan
1. Add `module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str` to the Module trait (no default — every impl must provide it)
2. Add `module_type: &'static str` field to `ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported`
3. Update the Display impl to print "{module_type} does not support {operation}"
4. Update default register_parameter/register_buffer to include module_type
5. Add `module_type_name()` to ALL existing Module implementations (~40+ structs):
   - Linear -> "Linear", Conv1d -> "Conv1d", Sequential -> "Sequential", etc.
6. Update any tests that construct or match ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported

## Testing requirements

### Unit tests (in ft-nn #[cfg(test)])

#### test_unsupported_register_includes_module_type
- Call register_parameter on a module that doesn't override it (e.g., ReLU)
- Assert the error message contains "ReLU"
- Assert the error message contains "register_parameter"

#### test_unsupported_register_buffer_includes_module_type
- Same for register_buffer on a leaf module

#### test_all_modules_have_type_name
- For each major module type (Linear, Conv1d, Conv2d, LSTM, Sequential, etc.):
  - Construct an instance
  - Assert module_type_name() returns a non-empty string
  - Assert module_type_name() does NOT contain "Module" alone (should be specific)

#### test_module_type_name_matches_struct_name
- For a selection of modules, verify that module_type_name() matches the Rust struct name
- E.g., Linear::module_type_name() == "Linear"

#### test_sequential_register_parameter_succeeds
- Verify that Sequential (which DOES support register_parameter) still works correctly
- Construct a Sequential, register a parameter, verify it appears in named_parameter_slots_own()

### E2E validation
- Run `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn` — all existing + new tests pass
- Run `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean

## Acceptance Criteria
- Error messages include the module type name (e.g., "ReLU does not support register_parameter")
- ALL Module implementations have module_type_name()
- 5+ new tests covering the enriched error paths
- Existing tests still pass
- Clippy clean

## Risk
Medium — this adds a required trait method to ~40 implementations. Use grep to find all `impl Module for` blocks. Each just needs one line added. Can be parallelized with subagents.

## Dependencies
None — isolated change within ft-nn.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:05.244308597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:31.066854625Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:31.066605804Z","close_reason":"ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported now carries module_type context, default Module registration methods report std::any::type_name::<Self>(), and ft-nn test plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-tnr","title":"Add logsumexp, dist, movedim, aminmax, unfold, renorm, isin","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:44:19.355981582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:59:22.971528996Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:59:22.971048236Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-tw0y","title":"Severed-autograd in linalg ops: cholesky_solve, linalg_inv, triangular_solve, slogdet, det","description":"After closing matrix_norm (frankentorch-i4rd), several other linalg ops in ft-api still extract values, run a kernel, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd pattern fixed under frankentorch-1tax for cov / corrcoef / mode etc.

Affected ops:
  - tensor_linalg_inv (lib.rs:11485) — d(A^{-1})/dA: grad_A = -A^{-T} grad_out A^{-T}. Composable via matmul + transpose + neg, with A^{-1} saved.
  - tensor_triangular_solve (lib.rs:11500) — d X / dB = A^{-T}, dX/dA contracted with grad gives an outer-product term masked to the triangular pattern.
  - tensor_cholesky_solve (lib.rs:11453) — chains through cholesky factor; gradient flows through the factor's triangular_solve calls.
  - tensor_linalg_det / tensor_linalg_slogdet — Jacobi formula: d log|det A| / dA = A^{-T}; det case: d det A / dA = det(A) * A^{-T}.

PyTorch's torch.linalg.{inv,solve_triangular,cholesky_solve,det,slogdet} are all differentiable. Surfaced during a /reality-check pass scanning for  and  patterns in linalg op bodies.

Each op gets its own child bead. Priority order based on usage frequency: linalg_inv → triangular_solve → cholesky_solve → det/slogdet.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:04:26.837996790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:04:26.837996790Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Update that line for consistency.","notes":"Made the legacy oracle cartography section consistent with the already-corrected scope wording by describing the local PyTorch mirror path as typical when present rather than fixed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:34:53.803252256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:35:11.784603527Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:35:11.783935432Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
